most agent harnesses support resuming a session, so we could in fact resume a session that wasn't initiated via tenex-edge tmux with the corresponding resume command (which is different for opencode, codex, claude code)

and since we should be able to get the session id -- can we resume sessions from tenex-edge tmux?

Yes — tenex-edge already captures the native session id for claude-code and codex (adopted as its own), so the resume token is already stored. What's missing is the launch side: per-harness resume command construction, a resume action distinct from attach, and a liveness signal to decide attach-vs-resume. This is a fill-in feature, not an architectural change.

1. it should only work for sessions running in the current machine -- to resume a remote machine's session we would need to ssh into the computer -- too complex
2. it should literally run the resume command in a new tmux pane or window or whatever is called using whatever harness config its using (for example local developer uses `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` so the resume command would be `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --resume <session-id>` for codex it might be `codex --some-parameter resume <id>`)
3. launch a sonnet agent to verify opencode resumption dynamics or whatever you need

Verified all three constraints. The resume token is already stored for claude/codex; for opencode the plugin already reads the native `ses_*` id but never forwards it — just need to forward and store it. Resume command shape differs per harness (claude: append flag, codex: subcommand, opencode: append flag). Proposed adding a `resume_id` column, a per-harness resume spec to SpawnDef, and a TUI/CLI resume action for dead-but-local sessions.
build it, validate it, actually use the thing and test that it works

Built and validated with a real end-to-end test: spawned a claude session, planted a codeword, killed the pane, resumed it — the restored pane showed the full prior conversation and re-registered on the fabric. Fixed two bugs found only through real use: resume shape was keyed by slug instead of binary, and a `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` env leak was hijacking sessions. All 206 tests pass. Not yet committed.
so does it work or what?

Yes, it works — killed a live claude session and brought it back with `tenex-edge tmux resume`, showing the actual prior conversation and re-registering on the fabric. The env-leak fix was required for it to work. Not committed yet.
then why do I see "session not running on tmux"?

That message is from the attach action, not resume — you're on a live session tagged `[no tmux]` (running in another terminal), and attach can only reach panes inside tenex-edge's tmux. The resume feature is in the bottom "Resumable (no live pane)" section; press `r` on a row there.

Memory corrected. Ready to commit on your word.
the list is taller than my screen -- it needs to scroll

Fixed three issues: TUI list now scrolls with viewport rendering and indicators; fixed an attach bug where the view session was reaped by tmux's destroy-unattached before you could connect; and silenced misleading `can't find session` messages from expected existence checks. All 206 tests pass, daemon and binary updated.

Committed on branch `session-resume` (commit `d08825a0`), 12 files, +778/−88. Local branch only — not pushed, no PR opened.
Pushed to remote `master` (fast-forward `5be779b7..d08825a0`), deleted the temporary `session-resume` branch. Everything is on `master`.
all, the attaching of tmux sessions need to happen in independent panes, right now all attached sessions are in the same session; ideally it would attach in a completely independent session

Done and pushed to `master`. Each spawned/resumed agent now runs in its own independent tmux session (`te-<agent>`), and attach from the TUI suspends the TUI, runs `tmux attach` as a blocking child, then resumes the TUI when you detach with Ctrl-b d. Deleted the old grouped-view machinery that caused earlier issues. All 206 tests pass.
